meaning: <意>n.格拉巴酒(用酒渣酿制的一种白兰地)
examples: The aged whiskies and musky grappa are made from local grapes like gewürztraminer, muscat and catawba.
The Nonino have revolutionized the way grappa is produced in Italy and sold throughout the world.
I got a little boy to go for a bottle of cognac but he came back and said he could only get grappa.
Try the George Burns, a cocktail made with two different kinds of scotch, plus grappa and bitters.
Sasha leaned against the tub beside him and took a tiny sip of grappa.
